evals.prompt: Build a Python script that creates a knowledge graph from a CSV of papers with columns: title, abstract, year, cited_paper_ids (comma-separated). The script should define a proper schema with labels and edge types, bulk-load the data, create a vector index on abstract embeddings, and then query for papers similar to 'attention mechanisms in transformers' using vector search, returning the top 5 with their citation counts.
evals.expected_output: A Python script using uni_db that: 1) defines Paper label with properties + embedding, 2) defines CITES edge type, 3) bulk loads from CSV, 4) creates vector index, 5) runs vector search + aggregation query

evals.prompt: Write a Rust program using uni-db that models a social network. Define User and Post labels, with FOLLOWS and LIKED edges. Load some sample data, then write queries to: find friends-of-friends for a given user, find the most liked posts, and detect 3-person follow cycles (A follows B follows C follows A).
evals.expected_output: A Rust program using the Uni crate with proper async setup, schema definition, sample data insertion, and three Cypher queries for friend-of-friend, most liked posts, and cycle detection

evals.prompt: I have a supply chain graph with Part and Product nodes connected by COMPONENT_OF edges. Some parts have a 'contaminated' flag. Write a Locy program in Python that propagates contamination: if any component part is contaminated, the parent product (and any product that uses that product as a sub-component) should also be flagged as risky. Then query all risky products and show which contaminated part caused them to be risky.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that: 1) creates schema for Part/Product with COMPONENT_OF edges, 2) loads sample data with some contaminated parts, 3) writes a Locy program with recursive contamination propagation rules, 4) queries the results showing risky products and root cause parts
